preamble: The transformation from a voiceless velar plosive /k/ to a voiced alveolar fricative /z/ is a complex phonetic shift involving changes in place, manner, and voicing. This shift is attested in the historical development of certain Indo-Iranian languages.
phoneticEffects: Fronting (velar to alveolar), Lenition (plosive to fricative), Voicing (voiceless to voiced)

examples.note: The Avestan voiceless velar plosive /k/ shifted to a voiced alveolar fricative /z/ in descendant Iranian languages like Pazend.
The Avestan voiceless velar plosive /k/ shifted to a voiced alveolar fricative /z/ in descendant Iranian languages like New Persian.

sources: Gray, L. H. (1902). Indo-Iranian Phonology: With Special Reference to the Middle and New Indo-Iranian Languages. Columbia University Press.
